message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Hong Kong has one of the longest life expectancies in the world but was heavily impacted by COVID-19 in 2022.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We constructed sex-specific life tables from 1998-2023 using parametric bootstrapping to account for statistical uncertainty in mortality rates. We used Arriaga’s decomposition method to estimate age-specific contributions to overall changes in life expectancy over this period. We also estimated cause-specific mortality rates.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Hong Kong reported 50666 deaths in 2020, 51354 in 2021, 63692 in 2022, and 54731 in 2023. Estimates of life expectancy in males and females in 2020 and 2021 were similar to the pre-pandemic trend from 1998-2019 but declined significantly in 2022. Life expectancy for males was 82.3 years in 2021 and 80.4 years in 2022, and for females it was 87.9 in 2021 and 86.4 years in 2022. Compared to the pre-pandemic trend, the 2022 values corresponded to reductions by 2.22 (95% CI: 2.08, 2.36) years in males and 2.30 (95% CI: 2.17, 2.43) years in females. The loss in life expectancy in 2022 was mainly attributed to increased respiratory mortality rates in adults aged 65 or above. In 2023 life expectancy increased by 0.60 (95% CI: 0.46, 0.75) years in males and by 1.10 (95% CI: 0.95, 1.26) years in females.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>In 2022 a very high respiratory mortality rate in older adults in Hong Kong during the COVID-19 pandemic was associated with a reduction in life expectancy by more than 2 years. In 2023 life expectancy increased towards the pre-pandemic trend.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Estimating changes in life expectancy in Hong Kong during the COVID-19 pandemic
